heading: Reporting requirement; amendment of certificate; revocation
label: 4014 Reporting requirement; amendment of certificate; revocation
notes: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as a note under .Oct. 8, 1982section 312 of Pub. L. 97–290section 4011 of this title

children.heading: Report of changes in matters specified; application to amend; treatment as application for issuance
children.label: a Report of changes in matters specified; application to amend; treatment as application for issuance
A
children.text: shall promptly report to the Secretary any change relevant to the matters specified in the certificate, and

children.label: B
children.text: may submit to the Secretary an application to amend the certificate to reflect the effect of the change on the conduct specified in the certificate.

An application for an amendment to a certificate of review shall be treated as an application for the issuance of a certificate. The effective date of an amendment shall be the date on which the application for the amendment is submitted to the Secretary.

children.heading: Request for compliance information; failure to provide; notice of noncompliance; revocation or modification; antitrust investigation; no civil investigative demand
children.label: b Request for compliance information; failure to provide; notice of noncompliance; revocation or modification; antitrust investigation; no civil investigative demand

children.text: If the Secretary or the Attorney General has reason to believe that the export trade, export trade activities, or methods of operation of a person holding a certificate of review no longer comply with the standards of , the Secretary shall request such information from such person as the Secretary or the Attorney General deems necessary to resolve the matter of compliance. Failure to comply with such request shall be grounds for revocation of the certificate under paragraph (2).section 4013(a) of this title

If the Secretary or the Attorney General determines that the export trade, export trade activities, or methods of operation of a person holding a certificate no longer comply with the standards of , or that such person has failed to comply with a request made under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall give written notice of the determination to such person. The notice shall include a statement of the circumstances underlying, and the reasons in support of, the determination. In the 60-day period beginning 30 days after the notice is given, the Secretary shall revoke the certificate or modify it as the Secretary or the Attorney General deems necessary to cause the certificate to apply only to the export trade, export trade activities, or methods of operation which are in compliance with the standards of .section 4013(a) of this titlesection 4013(a) of this title

For purposes of carrying out this subsection, the Attorney General, and the Assistant Attorney General in charge of the antitrust division of the Department of Justice, may conduct investigations in the same manner as the Attorney General and the Assistant Attorney General conduct investigations under , except that no civil investigative demand may be issued to a person to whom a certificate of review is issued if such person is the target of such investigation.section 1312 of this title
